articles.headline: 'Finally': Norway star Erling Haaland on fulfilling World Cup dream
articles.description: Erling Haaland tells ESPN he is ready to make his World Cup debut and show his love for Norway on football's biggest stage.

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.caption: Erling Haaland has 55 goals in 50 games for Norway.
images.credit: Jared C. Tilton - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions
images.caption: World Cup legends and celebrities reveal their tournament predictions

articles.headline: Jordan Henderson: Luka Modric is the best midfielder I've faced
articles.description: Jordan Henderson has praised Luka Modric as the greatest midfielder he has ever faced, ahead of England's match against Croatia on Wednesday.

images.name: Jordan Henderson Luka Modric [1296x729]
images.alt: Jordan Henderson challenges Luka Modric in the 2018 World Cup semifinal.

images.name: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener
images.caption: Henderson hails Modric as 'best' midfielder ahead of WC opener

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: New Zealand's match-predicting puppy poops on live TV
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Oli Sail, Tova O'Brien and Chris Chang with Bear the dog on TVNZ's Breakfast [1080x608]
Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany
images.caption: Lone Curaçao supporter in Berlin celebrates goal vs. Germany

articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.name: Belgium's Thibaut Courtois World Cup 2026 portrait [1296x729]
images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

images.name: Rod Stewart [1296x729]
images.alt: Rod Stewart celebrates at the game between Scotland and Haiti.
images.credit: (Photo by Martin Rickett/PA Images via Getty Images)

images.name: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston
images.caption: Scotland fans march to Fenway Park with bagpipes in Boston

articles.headline: Explained: How a chip in the ball allowed World Cup's fastest-ever goal by a sub to stand
articles.description: VAR overturned an offside decision after ruling there was a slight flick on the ball by Sweden's Alexander Isak, thanks to some technology in the ball.

images.name: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal [1296x729]
images.caption: Mattias Svanberg #19 of Sweden scores his team's fourth goal
images.credit: Manuel Velasquez - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win
images.caption: Potter: Isak-Gyokeres partnership showed quality in Tunisia win

article.headline: Dutch beat Brazil to claim bronze
article.description: Netherlands claimed their first third-placed finish at a World Cup as Brazil were brushed aside thanks to goals from Robin van Persie, Daley Blind and Georginio Wijnaldum.

images.name: Scolari: We have another final
images.caption: Luiz Felipe Scolari spoke to press ahead of Brazil's third place match on Saturday and reaffirmed his and his side's intention to finish the competition strong.

article.story:
Brazil were booed off the pitch in Brasilia as their disappointing World Cup campaign ended in another humiliating defeat.

Robin van Persie, Daley Blind and Georginio Wijnaldum gave the Netherlands an easy 3-0 win in a third-place match that was riddled with defensive errors from the hosts.

David Luiz, who performed poorly in Brazil's 7-1 semifinal defeat to Germany, gave another masterclass in how not to defend.

The Brazil vice-captain headed the ball straight to Blind in the first half and he lashed the ball past Julio Cesar for the Netherlands' second goal.

The match started in the worst possible way for the hosts, who went behind within three minutes thanks to Van Persie's penalty, and Wijnaldum wrapped up the latest humiliating defeat for Brazil in stoppage time.

After a poor first-half showing, Brazil improved slightly after the break, thanks mainly to the efforts of Oscar, who was booked for diving when he should have been awarded a penalty following a challenge by Blind.

Louis van Gaal's men were strong in defence all night, though. The same could not be said for the hosts, who were jeered after watching their team suffer back-to-back defeats on home soil for the first time since 1940.

Before kickoff, the Brazil supporters inside the stadium heeded Silva's pre-match call for them to get behind the team. The presence of the injured Neymar as a spectator on the bench also raised the spirits of the home fans as they took their seats. But all the good will disappeared very quickly.

With 87 seconds on the clock, Van Persie played Robben in behind the Brazil defence and Silva pulled the winger back, sending him tumbling to the floor.

The foul appeared to place well outside the box, but referee Djamel Haimoudi pointed to the spot. Much to the shock of the Dutch team, the Algerian official only produced a yellow card, even though the Brazil captain was the last man.

Van Persie took the ball and beat Julio Cesar. A stunned silence descended upon the Estadio Nacional in Brasilia.

Dismay turned to anger soon after as Holland doubled their lead thanks to some poor defending by Luiz. The defender headed backward, rather than over his own bar after a cross by Jonathan de Guzman.

The ball dropped straight to Blind, who took one touch and lashed the ball past Julio Cesar to register his first international goal.

Oscar looked to be Brazil's only hope of pulling a goal back. The Chelsea midfielder tested Jasper Cillessen, but then let himself down by diving to win a free kick, which Jo wasted by heading over.

When the halftime whistle came, the home fans booed loudly.

Scolari replaced Luis Gustavo with Fernandinho. Within nine minutes of his introduction, he was booked for a late tackle on Van Persie, who was left clutching his knee on the turf.

Another substitute, Hernanes, slid in on Robben, but he escaped a booking.

Oscar remained the hosts' best player. The midfielder found his Chelsea teammate Ramires with a clever pass but he dragged his shot wide under pressure from Ron Vlaar.

The Aston Villa captain handled Maicon's cross in the box just after the hour, but Haimoudi waved away appeals for a penalty.

The Algerian referee was at the centre of controversy again in the 67th minute when he booked Oscar for diving. Replays showed Blind had actually taken the Chelsea man out in the box. Indeed the clash of knees was so strong between the two players that Blind was carried off on a stretcher.

Van Gaal then thought his team should have had a penalty when Robben went down in the box under pressure from Fernandinho, but nothing was given.

The boos started five minutes from time as Brazil could not get the ball off the Dutch. And when Wijnaldum turned home Darryl Janmaat's cross in injury time the jeers become louder.

There was no lap of honour for the hosts as they trudged off down the tunnel with their heads bowed in shame.
